news.title: Surya Roshni's net profit dips 17.54% to ₹285.81 crore in FY26
news.para: Surya Roshni reported a 17.54% decline in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹285.81 crore, despite a 1.73% increase in total income to ₹7,594.39 crore. The company's managing director highlighted strong sales in March 2026 and a 7% revenue growth in lighting and consumer durables for FY26.

news.title: Shriram Properties' net profit up 30.41% to ₹100.81 crore in FY26
news.para: Shriram Properties witnessed a robust 30.41% surge in net profit for FY26, reaching ₹100.81 crore. Total income also climbed significantly by 39.40% to ₹1,356.93 crore. The company reported strong sales bookings of ₹2,354 crore and handed over 3,465 homes, while adding substantial new project pipelines.

news.title: Ajmera Realty's net profit up 18.93% to ₹149.79 crore in FY26
news.para: Ajmera Realty & Infra India reported a robust 18.93% rise in net profit for FY26, reaching ₹149.79 crore. Total income surged by 45.80% to ₹1,098 crore. The company highlighted a significant five-year growth trajectory and ambitious targets for FY27, driven by new projects and its Wadala land bank.

news.title: NBCC India's net profit rises 33.19% to ₹742.44 crore in FY26
news.para: NBCC (India) has announced a significant 33.19% surge in its net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹742.44 crore. This growth was accompanied by a 7.52% increase in total income to ₹13,195.88 crore. The company's board also recommended a final dividend of ₹0.46 per share for FY26.

news.title: Kalpataru signs cluster redevelopment project in Kandivali East, Mumbai
news.para: Kalpataru has secured a significant cluster redevelopment project in Mumbai's Ashok Nagar, encompassing five societies across 2.8 acres. This venture boasts a substantial free sale potential of 0.37 million sq ft carpet area, with an estimated gross development value reaching ₹1,250 crore. The company also reported robust financial performance in Q4 FY26, with pre-sales and collections showing impressive year-on-year growth.

news.title: Five listed REITs distribute ₹2,566 crore to unitholders in Q4 FY26
news.para: India’s five listed REITs distributed over ₹2,566 crore to more than 4.25 lakh unitholders in Q4 FY26. For FY26, cumulative distributions stood at over ₹8,900 crore, while the sector’s gross asset value crossed ₹2.72 lakh crore.

news.title: Puravankara acquires 14.57-acre land parcel in Mandur, Bengaluru
news.para: Puravankara has acquired a 14.57-acre land parcel in Bengaluru's Mandur, Budigere, with a potential gross development value of ₹2,300 crore. This acquisition, comprising both purchased land and a joint development agreement, will yield approximately 1.8 million sq ft of saleable area. The company's Bengaluru developable landbank now stands at 25.61 million sq ft.

news.title: No decision to dismantle LIFE Mission housing scheme: Kerala's LSG minister
news.para: Kerala's Local Self Government Minister K M Shaji has clarified that the UDF government has no plans to disband the LIFE Mission housing scheme. He stated that restoring powers to grama sabhas does not equate to scrapping the project, emphasizing that the new government aims to correct shortcomings rather than dismantle existing initiatives.

news.title: Bihar government seeks ₹1,600 crore from centre for rural housing
news.para: In a significant move to bolster rural development, Bihar has formally requested the Centre to disburse Rs 1,600 crore under the 'Mother Sanction' for the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana–Gramin. With the state's Single Nodal Account running low, timely support for beneficiaries is at risk.

news.title: Knowledge Realty Trust to invest ₹700 crore to build 1.4 million sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: Knowledge Realty Trust (KRT) plans to invest Rs 700 crore over three years to develop 1.4 million sq ft of office space in Bengaluru. The REIT, sponsored by Sattva Group and Blackstone, is also seeking acquisitions for inorganic growth. KRT anticipates continued strong performance in FY27, driven by demand from Global Capability Centres.

news.title: Buildings across city on Kolkata civic body's bulldozer list
news.para: Kolkata's civic body has issued 48-hour demolition notices to several 'unauthorised' buildings in Tiljala and Beleghata, leaving residents in distress. Many bought flats believing them to be legal, only to face displacement. Developers have vanished, leaving residents scrambling for answers and fearing for their families as authorities target 'red zones' with widespread illegal constructions.

news.title: SRA cracks down on 219 rehabilitation buildings in Mumbai without OC
news.para: The SRA has issued notices to over 200 buildings lacking Occupancy Certificates (OC) that have been handed over to slum dwellers. This crackdown follows the state government's amnesty scheme, aiming to regularize such structures and address issues like lack of maintenance funds and developers forcing early possession.

news.title: Supreme court's rule on OCs delays smart meter rollout in Karnataka
news.para: Karnataka's smart meter rollout faced significant delays due to confusion over mandatory occupancy certificates. While aimed at modernizing the power sector, the Supreme Court's order stalled new connections, impacting lakhs of applications. Recent government intervention has accelerated installations, with over three lakh smart meters now in place, though challenges persist for many awaiting connections.

news.title: Municipality identifies 49 unsafe buildings in Mapusa
news.para: Mapusa authorities have identified 49 structurally unsound buildings, including 42 private and 7 government structures, within municipal limits. A joint re-inspection drive, ordered by the North Goa district collector, flagged these buildings as dangerous ahead of the monsoon.

news.title: Bengaluru civic bodies' revenue drops under khata conversion scheme
news.para: Bengaluru's civic bodies face a projected revenue loss of nearly Rs 1,015 crore due to a fee reduction for converting B-khata to A-khata properties. The "Nanna Khata Nanna Hakku" campaign offers a 100-day window to regularize properties at a reduced 2% guidance value.

news.title: Drain-top buildings to face higher tax in Sambhajinagar; crackdown on direct sewage discharge
news.para: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar municipal corporation will raise property taxes for buildings constructed over major drains. This move aims to tackle monsoon flooding and improve desilting operations. The civic body also ordered strict action against direct sewage discharge into stormwater drains. Enforcement drives will ensure properties connect to sewage treatment plants.

news.title: Dehradun cancels registration of 103 homestays violating rules
news.para: Dehradun district administration has cancelled registrations of 103 homestays for violating safety norms and operational guidelines. Inspections revealed commercialization, illegal bars, and lack of basic facilities, leading to the crackdown following a surge in criminal activities. The administration is also removing these properties from the state tourism website.

news.title: Delhi to overhaul property registration system, modernise sub-registrar offices: CM
news.para: The Delhi government is preparing to completely overhaul the property registration system in the capital, aiming to make Sub-Registrar offices more modern, transparent and citizen-friendly, with a focus on eliminating delays, corruption and dependence on agents.

news.title: Lucknow development body to redevelop dilapidated complex in Indira Nagar at ₹110 crore
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is set to redevelop the 40-year-old Kailashkunj Commercial Complex into a 13-storey mixed-use high-rise. The project, estimated at Rs 110 crore, will feature commercial spaces on lower floors and 3BHK residential flats above. Allottees will receive 1.25 times their current area in the new modern complex.

news.title: Chandigarh plans vertical shift under master plan 2031
news.para: Chandigarh's Master Plan 2031 proposes significant changes, allowing higher density, taller buildings, and greater design flexibility.  These amendments aim for vertical growth and optimized land use, contrasting with previous restrictive norms.  The changes include increased FAR, relaxed height restrictions, and adjusted ground coverage across various land-use categories.
